structure.title: Rural Development Single Family Housing Guaranteed Loan Program
structure.summary: The document is a USDA Rural Development Guaranteed Housing Program income limits table for FY 2024. It explains state and county-based income eligibility limits for the Single Family Housing Guaranteed Loan Program, showing adjusted income limits by household size (1 to 8 persons) and income category, including very low income, low income, and moderate income guaranteed loan limits. The excerpt specifically lists multiple Alabama metro areas and HUD metro FMR areas, with detailed income thresholds for places such as Anniston-Oxford-Jacksonville, Auburn-Opelika, Birmingham-Hoover, Chilton County, Columbus (GA-AL), Daphne-Fairhope-Foley, Decatur, and Dothan. It also includes notes on how to calculate limits for households larger than 8 persons and defines moderate income.

The partial pages shown cover multiple Alabama metropolitan and HUD metro FMR areas such as Anniston-Oxford-Jacksonville, Auburn-Opelika, Birmingham-Hoover, Columbus (GA-AL), Daphne-Fairhope-Foley, Decatur, Dothan, Florence-Muscle Shoals, Gadsden, Huntsville, Mobile, Montgomery, and Tuscaloosa-related areas. It also includes footnotes explaining how income limits are calculated for households larger than eight people and how moderate income is defined.

nodes.title: GUARANTEED HOUSING PROGRAM INCOME LIMITS

nodes.title: GUARANTEED HOUSING PROGRAM INCOME LIMITS
nodes.summary:
The partial document is a table from HB-1-3555 Appendix 5 showing FY 2024 adjusted income limits for the Guaranteed Housing Program. It lists income thresholds by household size (1 to 8 persons) for different counties and areas.

The Alabama portion includes Walker, Wilcox, and Winston Counties, with categories for Very Low Income, Low Income, and Moderate Income Guaranteed Loan limits. The Alaska portion lists several metro and non-metro areas, including Anchorage MSA, Matanuska-Susitna Borough, Fairbanks MSA, and Aleutians East Borough. For Alaska, separate limits are given for areas with a road system and without a road system, with categories for RHS Very Low Income, RHS Low Income, and RHS Moderate Income Guaranteed Loan.

The document also includes explanatory footnotes: household limits for more than 8 persons are increased by 8% of the 4-person limit per additional person, and moderate income is defined using a formula based on median family income and low-income limits.

nodes.title: GUARANTEED HOUSING PROGRAM INCOME LIMITS

For each area, it provides income thresholds for RHS Very Low Income, RHS Low Income, and RHS Moderate Income-Guaranteed Loan, with separate limits for areas with a road system and areas without a road system. The pages also include notes explaining how Alaska home ownership income limits are applied, how to calculate limits for households larger than 8 persons, and how moderate income is defined.

nodes.title: GUARANTEED HOUSING PROGRAM INCOME LIMITS

The pages shown are part of a longer document and include page numbers and publication/date information.

nodes.title: GUARANTEED HOUSING PROGRAM INCOME LIMITS
nodes.summary:
The partial document is a table from USDA Rural Housing Service guidance showing FY 2024 adjusted income limits for the Guaranteed Housing Program in Alaska. It lists multiple Alaska counties/boroughs and census areas, including Kodiak Island Borough, Kusilvak Census Area, Lake and Peninsula Borough, Nome Census Area, North Slope Borough, Northwest Arctic Borough, Petersburg Borough, and Prince of Wales-Hyder Census Area.

For each area, the document provides income limits by household size from 1 to 8 persons and separates the limits into:
- Areas with a road system (115%)
- Areas without a road system (150%)

It presents three program categories for each household size:
- RHS Very Low Income
- RHS Low Income
- RHS Moderate Income – Guaranteed Loan

The document also includes notes explaining that Alaska Home Ownership very low and low income median figures apply to RHS programs, a rule for adding 8% of the 4-person limit for each person above 8, and a definition of moderate income. It appears as pages 12 and 13 of a larger 334-page document.

nodes.title: GUARANTEED HOUSING PROGRAM INCOME LIMITS
nodes.summary:
This document is a section of the USDA Rural Housing Service “Guaranteed Housing Program Income Limits” for Alaska, showing FY 2024 adjusted income limits by household size for multiple Alaska jurisdictions.

Main points covered:
- Income limits are listed for several Alaska areas, including:
  - North Slope Borough
  - Northwest Arctic Borough
  - Petersburg Borough
  - Prince of Wales-Hyder Census Area
  - Sitka City and Borough
  - Skagway Municipality
  - Southeast Fairbanks Census Area
  - Wrangell City and Borough
- For each area, limits are broken out by:
  - Household size from 1 to 8 persons
  - Program category:
    - RHS Very Low Income
    - RHS Low Income
    - RHS Moderate Income – Guaranteed Loan
- Each area also has separate calculations for:
  - Areas with a road system (115%)
  - Areas without a road system (150%)
- The pages include notes explaining:
  - Alaska Home Ownership VLI and LI median incomes apply to RHS programs and may not apply to LIHTC or HUD programs
  - For households larger than 8, add 8% of the 4-person limit for each additional person
  - Moderate income is defined using specific federal and area income comparison rules
- The excerpt is part of a larger appendix and includes page numbering and publication/date information.

It lists income thresholds by area, including several MSAs and counties, and breaks them down by household size from 1 person through 8 persons. For each location, it provides three categories: Very Low Income, Low Income, and Moderate Income for Guaranteed Loans. The excerpt includes areas such as Dubuque, Iowa City, Omaha-Council Bluffs, Sioux City, Waterloo-Cedar Falls, and multiple Iowa counties like Adams, Allamakee, Appanoose, Audubon, Buchanan, Buena Vista, Butler, Calhoun, and Carroll. It also includes footnotes explaining how to calculate limits for households larger than eight and how moderate income is defined.

- The pages shown are part of a larger document, with page numbers 193 and 194 of 334.

It also includes footnotes explaining that the 8-person limit increases by 8% of the 4-person limit for each additional person beyond eight, and that moderate income is defined by a formula based on median family income and area low-income limits.

nodes.title: GUARANTEED HOUSING PROGRAM INCOME LIMITS
nodes.summary: This partial document is a USDA “Guaranteed Housing Program Income Limits” table for Wisconsin for FY 2024. It lists adjusted income limits by household size (1 to 8 persons) for three categories: Very Low Income, Low Income, and Moderate Income for Guaranteed Loans. The excerpt covers several Wisconsin metropolitan areas and counties, including La Crosse-Onalaska, Madison-area counties and metro FMR areas, Milwaukee-Waukesha-West Allis, Minneapolis-St. Paul-Bloomington (WI portion), Oshkosh-Neenah, Racine, Sheboygan, Wausau-Weston, and multiple counties such as Adams, Ashland, Barron, Bayfield, and Buffalo. It also includes footnotes explaining how the 8-person limit is calculated and how moderate income is defined.

nodes.title: GUARANTEED HOUSING PROGRAM INCOME LIMITS
nodes.summary: This partial document is a table from HUD’s “Guaranteed Housing Program Income Limits” for Wisconsin, showing FY 2024 adjusted income limits by household size. It lists multiple areas in Wisconsin, including MSAs and counties such as Racine, Sheboygan, Wausau-Weston, Adams, Ashland, Barron, Bayfield, and Buffalo. For each area, it provides income thresholds for very low income, low income, and moderate income guaranteed loan categories across 1 to 8 person households. The document also includes footnotes explaining how the 8-person limit is calculated and defines moderate income.

nodes.title: GUARANTEED HOUSING PROGRAM INCOME LIMITS
nodes.summary: The partial document is a HUD “Guaranteed Housing Program Income Limits” table for Wisconsin for FY 2024. It lists adjusted income limits by county and household size (1 to 8 persons) for three program categories: Very Low Income, Low Income, and Moderate-Income Guaranteed Loan. The excerpt covers counties such as Burnett, Clark, Crawford, Dodge, Door, Dunn, Florence, Forest, Grant, Green Lake, Iron, Jackson, Jefferson, Juneau, Lafayette, Langlade, Manitowoc, and Marinette. Most counties shown share the same income thresholds, though some have higher limits due to local area conditions. The document also includes footnotes explaining how to calculate limits for households larger than eight and how “moderate income” is defined.

nodes.title: GUARANTEED HOUSING PROGRAM INCOME LIMITS
nodes.summary: This partial document is a table from USDA HB-1-3555, Appendix 5 showing FY 2024 adjusted income limits for the Guaranteed Housing Program in Wisconsin. It lists income thresholds by household size (1 to 8 persons) for several counties, including Green Lake, Iron, Jackson, Jefferson, Juneau, Lafayette, Langlade, Manitowoc, and Marinette. For each county, it provides three categories of limits: Very Low Income, Low Income, and Moderate Income for Guaranteed Loans. Most counties shown share the same limits, while some, such as Jefferson and Lafayette Counties, have higher values. The footnotes explain how the 8-person limit is adjusted for larger households and define how moderate income is calculated.

nodes.title: GUARANTEED HOUSING PROGRAM INCOME LIMITS
nodes.summary:
This partial document is a USDA Guaranteed Housing Program income limits table for Wisconsin for FY 2024. It lists county-by-county adjusted income limits for several household sizes (1 through 8 persons) and three income categories: Very Low Income, Low Income, and Moderate Income for Guaranteed Loans. The excerpt covers multiple Wisconsin counties, including Marquette, Menominee, Monroe, Oneida, Pepin, Polk, Portage, Price, Richland, Rusk, Sauk, Sawyer, Shawano, Taylor, Trempealeau, Vernon, Vilas, and Walworth. 

The table shows that income limits vary by county and household size, with higher limits generally for larger households and certain counties having higher thresholds than others. It also includes footnotes explaining how to calculate the limit for households larger than 8 persons and how moderate income is defined.

nodes.title: GUARANTEED HOUSING PROGRAM INCOME LIMITS
nodes.summary:
This partial document is a USDA-style table of FY 2024 adjusted income limits for the Guaranteed Housing Program in Wisconsin. It lists county-by-county income thresholds for Rusk, Sauk, Sawyer, Shawano, Taylor, Trempealeau, Vernon, Vilas, Walworth, Washburn, Waupaca, Waushara, and Wood counties.

For each county, the table provides income limits by household size from 1 to 8 persons for three categories: Very Low Income, Low Income, and Moderate Income for guaranteed loans. Most counties shown share the same standard limits, while some counties such as Sauk, Trempealeau, Vernon, Walworth, and Waupaca have higher county-specific limits.

The document also includes footnotes explaining that households larger than 8 persons may add 8% of the 4-person limit for each additional person, and it defines how moderate income is calculated. Page numbers and revision/date information are shown at the bottom.

nodes.title: GUARANTEED HOUSING PROGRAM INCOME LIMITS
nodes.summary: This excerpt is from HUD’s Guaranteed Housing Program Income Limits appendix for FY 2024. It lists adjusted income limits by household size (1 to 8 persons) for specific counties and metropolitan areas in Wisconsin and Wyoming. For each area, it provides three categories: Very Low Income, Low Income, and Moderate Income for guaranteed loans. The table includes footnotes explaining how limits are adjusted for households larger than 8 people and how moderate income is defined.

nodes.title: GUARANTEED HOUSING PROGRAM INCOME LIMITS
nodes.summary: This partial document is a table from HUD’s HB-1-3555 Appendix 5 listing FY 2024 adjusted income limits for the Guaranteed Housing Program in Wyoming. It breaks down income eligibility by county and metropolitan area, and shows limits for households of 1 through 8 persons under three categories: Very Low Income, Low Income, and Moderate Income for Guaranteed Loans. It includes entries for areas such as Casper MSA, Cheyenne MSA, and multiple Wyoming counties, with footnotes explaining how limits are adjusted for households larger than eight and how moderate income is defined.

nodes.title: GUARANTEED HOUSING PROGRAM INCOME LIMITS
nodes.summary: This partial document is a federal housing program income limits table for Wyoming, specifically for the FY 2024 Adjusted Income Limits under the Guaranteed Housing Program (HB-1-3555, Appendix 5). It lists county-by-county income thresholds for different household sizes (1 to 8 persons) across three categories: Very Low Income, Low Income, and Moderate Income for Guaranteed Loans. The excerpt covers several Wyoming counties, including Goshen, Hot Springs, Johnson, Lincoln, Niobrara, Park, Platte, Sheridan, Sublette, Sweetwater, Teton, Uinta, Washakie, and Weston. It also includes notes explaining how to calculate income limits for households larger than 8 people and how moderate income is defined.

nodes.title: GUARANTEED HOUSING PROGRAM INCOME LIMITS
nodes.summary: This excerpt from HB-1-3555, Appendix 5 lists FY 2024 adjusted income limits for USDA Guaranteed Housing Program eligibility. It covers Wyoming counties (Sweetwater, Teton, Uinta, Washakie, and Weston) and American Samoa, showing income thresholds for 1- to 8-person households under three categories: Very Low Income, Low Income, and Moderate Income (Guaranteed Loan). It also includes footnotes explaining how to calculate limits for households larger than 8 people and how moderate income is defined.

nodes.title: GUARANTEED HOUSING PROGRAM INCOME LIMITS
nodes.summary: The document presents FY 2024 adjusted income limits for the USDA Guaranteed Housing Program for two U.S. territories: American Samoa and Guam. It lists income thresholds by household size from 1 to 8 persons for three categories: very low income, low income, and moderate income for guaranteed loans. For American Samoa, the limits are lower than Guam’s across all categories, with notes explaining that for households larger than 8, the 8-person limit is increased by 8% per additional person. It also includes a definition of moderate income based on the greater of several income benchmarks.

nodes.title: GUARANTEED HOUSING PROGRAM INCOME LIMITS
nodes.summary: This partial document shows FY 2024 adjusted income limits for the USDA Guaranteed Housing Program for two U.S. territories: Guam and the Northern Mariana Islands. It lists income thresholds by household size from 1 to 8 persons for three categories: very low income, low income, and moderate income for guaranteed loans. The document also includes a note that for households over 8 persons, the 8-person limit increases by 8% for each additional person, and it explains how moderate income is defined.

nodes.title: GUARANTEED HOUSING PROGRAM INCOME LIMITS
nodes.summary: The partial document is a section of HUD’s “Guaranteed Housing Program Income Limits” for FY 2024. It lists adjusted income limits by household size for specific U.S. territories and Puerto Rico areas. The first part shows the Northern Mariana Islands with income thresholds for very low income, low income, and moderate income guaranteed loan eligibility. The second part covers Puerto Rico, including multiple metropolitan and HUD metro fair market rent areas such as Aguadilla-Isabela, Arecibo, Guayama, Mayagüez, Ponce, San Germán, and parts of the San Juan-Bayamón-Caguas region. For each area, the document provides income limits for 1 to 8 person households across the same three program categories. It also includes footnotes explaining how to calculate limits for households larger than eight and how moderate income is defined.

nodes.title: GUARANTEED HOUSING PROGRAM INCOME LIMITS
nodes.summary: The partial document is a HUD income limits table for the Guaranteed Housing Program in Puerto Rico for FY 2024. It lists adjusted income limits by household size (1 to 8 persons) for multiple metropolitan and nonmetropolitan areas, including Aguadilla-Isabela, Arecibo, Guayama, Mayagüez, Ponce, San Germán, several San Juan metro subareas, Yauco, and the Puerto Rico HUD Nonmetro Area. For each area, it shows thresholds for Very Low Income, Low Income, and Moderate-Income Guaranteed Loan categories. The document also includes notes explaining how to calculate limits for households larger than eight persons and how moderate income is defined, along with page and revision information.

nodes.title: GUARANTEED HOUSING PROGRAM INCOME LIMITS
nodes.summary: The document is a USDA/FHA-style reference table for Guaranteed Housing Program income limits in Puerto Rico for FY 2024. It lists adjusted income limits by household size (1 through 8 persons) for three geographic areas: the San Juan-Guaynabo HUD Metro FMR Area, the Yauco MSA, and the Puerto Rico HUD Nonmetro Area. For each area, it provides thresholds for Very Low Income, Low Income, and Moderate Income (for guaranteed loans). The page also includes notes explaining how limits for households over 8 persons are calculated and how moderate income is defined.

nodes.title: GUARANTEED HOUSING PROGRAM INCOME LIMITS
nodes.summary: This page presents FY 2024 adjusted income limits for the Guaranteed Housing Program in the U.S. Virgin Islands. It lists income thresholds by household size (1 to 8 persons) for three island areas: St. Croix, St. John, and St. Thomas. For each area, it shows limits for Very Low Income, Low Income, and Moderate Income for guaranteed loans. The page also includes footnotes explaining that the 8-person limit is increased by 8% for each additional person beyond 8, and defines how moderate income is calculated.
